title: Creamy Smoked Yakisoba Bake
description: A crowd-pleasing Malaysian dish featuring a creamy fermented base with birds-eye chili, baked to perfection and kosher-friendly.
instructions: Spread the fermented tofu paste evenly over one side of a single sheet of yakisoba.
Top with smoked salmon slices, sprinkling the bird's eye chili flakes and ginger mixture over it.
Drizzle soy sauce, salt, and white pepper powder over the salmon and paste combination. Drizzle olive oil on top for extra flavor.
Place another sheet of yakisoba directly on top of the layers without touching the filling to prevent burning.
Brush the entire surface with a little bit of olive oil before baking.
Bake in preheated oven for about 15 minutes until edges are golden brown and crispy.
Serve warm as an excellent dinner option.
